blocker.summary: transformers 5.13.0 qwen3_5_moe uses fused expert tensors (experts.gate_up_proj); the checkpoint stores 256 per-expert compressed-tensors modules per layer (weight_packed/scale/shape/zero_point, asymmetric INT4 group-32). The loader runs an unconditional DecompressExperts weight conversion that (1) fails with AssertionError: Asymmetric quant requires zero-point values although zero-points exist in the ckpt, and (2) decompresses INT4 to BF16 during load, which cannot fit the 44 GiB two-GPU gate by construction.
evidence: DecompressExperts on tensors destined for model.layers.39.mlp.experts.gate_up_proj. Ckpt contains: 6
AssertionError: Asymmetric quant requires zero-point values
blocker.first_attempt_note: initial load also hit an allocator-fragmentation OOM, fixed with PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True; the conversion failure above is the real blocker

per_steer_options_for_operator: A: attempt a different transformers/compressed-tensors version pair whose qwen3_5_moe path runs per-expert compressed modules without conversion (risk: research venv churn; the M22-M35 stack depends on transformers 5.13)
B: vLLM/SGLang load path — steer permits only if it exposes equivalent router-logit telemetry and hooks (vLLM does not by default -> black-box arm)
C: different checkpoint identity (operator decision per steer; no silent switch)
service_state_after: GPUs freed (50/18 MiB); ollama up; llama-server processes present
